affected_parts: Older leaves, stems, and sometimes fruits/tubers
favorable_conditions: Warm temperatures with repeated leaf wetness and humidity.
diagnosis_notes: Dark concentric lesions on older leaves with surrounding yellowing are common indicators.
disease_description: Early Blight is a foliar fungal disease that can reduce canopy health and final yield if unmanaged.
symptoms: Brown to dark lesions with ring patterns, progressive chlorosis, and lower canopy decline.
causes: The disease is typically caused by Alternaria pathogens surviving in debris, volunteer plants, and infected seed material.
prevention_methods: Use clean planting material, remove infected debris, rotate crops, and reduce prolonged leaf wetness.
treatment_methods: Prune heavily affected tissue and apply crop-approved fungicide programs with resistance rotation.
treatment_organic: Mulch soil, improve airflow, and use copper/sulfur-based products where permitted.
treatment_chemical: Use contact and systemic fungicides per crop label and local extension guidance.
recovery_time: 2-4 weeks if intervention starts before severe canopy collapse.
monitoring_tips: Inspect lower leaves first and track lesion spread weekly.
